conversion_factor_ratio_equal_to_1_e_g: Conversion factor = ratio equal to 1 (e.g., 1 ft = 12 in → 12 in / 1 ft). Multiplying by it changes the unit without changing the value. SI prefixes (kilo, milli) are powers of 10 — just shift the decimal.
length_mass_volume_temperature_time_en: Length, mass, volume, temperature, time, energy, pressure. The single rule that matters: track units through every step. If your final answer is in m² when you wanted seconds, you made an error somewhere upstream.
daily_life_conversions_f_c_mph_km_h_lb: Daily-life conversions (°F↔°C, mph↔km/h, lb↔kg) plus AP / SAT / lab measurements. Each question shows the conversion factor after answering so you build the muscle memory.
real_world_unit_conversion_scenarios_g: Real-world unit-conversion scenarios: gas mileage, recipe scaling, dosage calculations, travel-distance estimates. Word problems are 80% reading comprehension + 20% conversion math.
every_si_prefix_is_a_power_of_10_slide: Every SI prefix is a power of 10. Slide the source and target exponents to feel how far apart scales really are — the core skill behind scientific notation and Fermi estimates.
question: 🧠 Question
click_generate_for_an_ai_crafted_word_: Click Generate for an AI-crafted word problem
adjust_source_and_target_exponents_10_: Adjust source and target exponents (10^N). Widget classifies magnitude change into 5 discrete bands. No score, no reveal.
hypothesis_what_real_world_examples_sp: Hypothesis: What real-world examples span each magnitude band?
compare_cm_km_mm_light_year_how_many_o: Compare cm→km, mm→light-year. How many OOM each?
why_are_scientists_trained_in_orders_o: Why are scientists trained in orders of magnitude?
i_understand_explain_in_own_words: I understand — explain in own words
explain_why_dimensional_reasoning_acro: Explain why dimensional reasoning across many OOM is hard.
design_note_discrete_5_state_magnitude: Design note: discrete 5-state magnitude marker; no error score; no reveal — by design.
metric_prefixes_powers_of_10_from_atom: 🔬 Metric Prefixes — Powers of 10 from atoms to galaxies
